01_initial_request.target_name: MSC-1DCNN (multiscale, multichannel, one-dimensional convolutional neural network)
01_initial_request.input_data: Worldwide empirical slope data, slope influencing factors, and soil or rock parameters (variability of cohesion c and friction angle φ)
01_initial_request.user_intent: To improve the accuracy of slope stability state prediction and reduce algorithm complexity while addressing the variability of soil or rock parameters
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: High prediction accuracy for slope stability states and calculated probability of failure

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Slope stability prediction requires empirical data but the number of slope cases limits accuracy. [Gap] Only 108 slope cases collected from literature, insufficient for robust prediction. [Decision] To achieve improved prediction accuracy, chose to use data amplification method to perform dataset expansion on original slope cases, expecting to obtain 540 groups of amplified data.

parameters.amplification_rule: c and φ simultaneously and continuously increased or decreased by 10%, 20%, 30%, 40%, and 50%

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Amplified data consist of 540 groups, including 185 groups of slope failure data and 355 groups of slope safety data

references.citation: Li et al. 2012; Gökceoglu and Aksoy 1996
references.relevance: Provides basis for data amplification rule showing c and φ are positively correlated with safety factor
Data amplification idea originates from image identification field

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Dataset division and normalization affect prediction method performance. [Gap] Need to determine optimal preprocessing configuration. [Decision] To achieve stable learning and improved accuracy, chose to use data normalization to perform standardization on input data, expecting to obtain normalized dataset with reduced fluctuations.

parameters.normalization_method: subtracting the mean and dividing by the variance

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Under different dataset division conditions, prediction index A and F1 value improved after data normalization; 4:1 ratio produces better prediction results

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Basic 1DCNN has limitations in prediction ability. [Gap] Need to improve network structure to balance hierarchical depth and width. [Decision] To achieve improved prediction performance and reduced computational burden, chose to use Multiscale Parallel Module (MSM) to perform multiscale convolution learning on input feature matrices, expecting to obtain enhanced feature extraction capability.

parameters.process: inputs simultaneously convolved by convolution layers of different scale convolution kernels, feature matrices summed by add layer, activated by activation function, dropout layer applied

02_agent_trajectory.observation: MS-1DCNN had more than 1.1%, 0%, 1.59%, and 0.8% improvements in A, P, R, and F1 compared with 1DCNN

references.citation: Cai et al. 2016; Deng et al. 2019
references.relevance: Supports multiscale convolution layers for improved learning efficiency

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Single-scale convolution limits feature extraction. [Gap] Need to increase channel dimension for lighter training parameters. [Decision] To achieve expanded model width with controlled parameters, chose to use Multichannel Parallel Module (MCM) to perform multichannel parallel convolution on input feature matrices, expecting to obtain increased channel dimension with reduced computational cost.

parameters.process: different channel convolution layers convolve and activate input feature matrices, output concatenated in channel dimension, dropout layer applied
parameters.kernel_size_1_layer: convolutional layer with kernel size of 1 to limit computational parameters

02_agent_trajectory.observation: MC-1DCNN improved A, P, R, and F1 by 1.1%, 1.64%, 0.05%, and 0.82% respectively compared with 1DCNN

references.relevance: Supports multichannel parallel convolution for inception-based architecture
Supports 1-scale convolution layer for reducing training parameters

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] MSC-1DCNN architecture combines MSM and MCM modules. [Gap] Need to configure network training parameters. [Decision] To achieve model training with reduced overfitting, chose to use Dropout layer to perform regularization during CNN training, expecting to obtain robust network parameters.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Dropout reduces complex coadaptations of neurons, enabling learning of more robust properties

references.relevance: Original dropout method proposal with parameter 0.5

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Activation functions are key to feature selection and nonlinear mapping. [Gap] Need to determine optimal activation function for MSC-1DCNN. [Decision] To achieve best classification performance, chose to use tanh activation function to perform nonlinear mapping on extracted features, expecting to obtain higher evaluation indices.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Evaluation indices A, P, R, and F1 of MSC-1DCNN with tanh activation function were higher compared with ReLU and sigmoid

references.relevance: Supports activation function importance for convolution layers

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Different optimizers have different effects on deep learning models. [Gap] Need to select optimal optimizer for MSC-1DCNN. [Decision] To achieve best optimization performance, chose to use Adam optimizer to perform network weight parameter adjustment, expecting to obtain minimized error and optimal parameter matrix.

parameters.tested_optimizers: SGD, RMSprop, Nadam, Adagrad, Adadelta, Adam

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Adam and Adadelta as optimizer were better than other optimizers; Adam was selected for MSC-1DCNN

references.relevance: Supports optimizer selection as critical part of building and testing machine learning models

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Need to verify MSC-1DCNN superiority over existing methods. [Gap] Performance comparison with state-of-the-art methods required. [Decision] To achieve validation of method advancement, chose to use comparative analysis to perform performance evaluation against SVM, ANN, ELM, and 1DCNN, expecting to obtain superior prediction accuracy.

parameters.evaluation_indices: Accuracy (A), Precision (P), Recall rate (R), F1 score

02_agent_trajectory.observation: A and F1 of MSC-1DCNN are better than other prediction methods for both original and amplified data; MSC-1DCNN suitable for both large and small dataset training

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Variability of soil or rock parameters causes uncertainty in slope stability prediction. [Gap] Need to address uncertainty from poorly known parameters. [Decision] To achieve probability-based stability evaluation, chose to use joint probability distribution sampling to perform random sampling from c and φ distributions, expecting to obtain 1×10^4 samples for failure probability calculation.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 1×10^4 samplings randomly selected; distributions of c and φ obtained

references.relevance: Supports lognormal distribution assumption for c and φ

references.citation: Vanmarcke 1997; Oka and Wu 1990; Liang et al. 1990
references.relevance: Supports probability analysis method for slope stability evaluation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Case slope in Shao Jiazhuang village requires stability evaluation. [Gap] Need to apply MSC-1DCNN to real-world slope with parameter variability. [Decision] To achieve practical slope stability prediction, chose to use MSC-1DCNN to perform stability state prediction on case slope with 1×10^4 samples, expecting to obtain failure probability and stability boundary.

parameters.case_location: Shao Jiazhuang village, Guizhou Province, China

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Slope is unstable; failure probability is 85.93%; boundary of c and φ distributions causing failure described

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Comparative analysis with SVM, ANN, ELM, 1DCNN and case study application with probability analysis

accuracy_improvement.value: A and F1 highest compared with SVM, ANN, ELM, 1DCNN
accuracy_improvement.interpretation: MSC-1DCNN achieves superior prediction accuracy over state-of-the-art methods

msm_improvement.unit: percentage improvement in A, P, R, F1
msm_improvement.interpretation: Multiscale parallel convolution improves evaluation index R significantly

mcm_improvement.unit: percentage improvement in A, P, R, F1
mcm_improvement.interpretation: Multicore parallel convolution promotes classification performance, especially index P

failure_probability.interpretation: Case slope failure probability calculated considering variability of soil parameters

prediction_time.interpretation: MSC-1DCNN prediction time significantly lower than GEOstudio (5s) and ABAQUS (40s)

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved: MSC-1DCNN demonstrates high prediction accuracy for slope stability states (superior to SVM, ANN, ELM, 1DCNN), reduced algorithm complexity (0.06s prediction time vs 5-40s for traditional methods), and successfully addresses variability of soil/rock parameters through probability of failure calculation (85.93% for case study)
